info.title: Urantia Papers API
info.description: A developer and AI-agent friendly API for the Urantia Papers. Provides full-text search, structured content access, and audio URLs for all 17,000+ paragraphs across 197 papers.

post.description: Pass any paragraph reference format. If already bookmarked, updates the category and returns 200.

ref.description: Paragraph reference in any format: globalId (1:2.0.1), standardReferenceId (2:0.1), or paperSectionParagraphId (2.0.1)

200.description: Bookmark already exists (updated category if provided)

get.summary: List bookmark categories with counts and refs

delete.summary: Delete a bookmark by paragraph reference

post.description: Pass any paragraph reference format. Multiple notes per paragraph are allowed. Format: 'plain' (default) or 'markdown'.

get.summary: Get reading progress summary per paper with refs and completion

post.description: Pass an array of paragraph references in any format. Already-read paragraphs are silently skipped.

delete.description: Returns 204 whether or not progress existed. Returns 404 only if the paragraph ref is invalid.

get.summary: List OAuth apps owned by the authenticated user
Check if the user has already consented to an app's scopes

post.summary: Upload an app logo (owner-only, max 2MB, PNG/JPEG/WebP)

get.summary: Check if the authenticated user is an admin
Get the full table of contents
get.description: Returns the complete table of contents with parts and their papers. This is typically the first endpoint an AI agent should call to understand the book structure.
Returns metadata for all papers in the Urantia Book, ordered by paper number.

get.summary: Get a paper with all its paragraphs
get.description:
Returns a single paper's metadata along with all its paragraphs in order. Paper IDs range from 0 (Foreword) to 196.

Use `?include=entities` to include typed entity mentions in each paragraph.
Returns all sections for a given paper, ordered by section number.

Returns a single random paragraph from the Urantia Book. Useful for daily quotes or exploration.

Use `?include=entities` to include typed entity mentions in the response.

Use `?minLength=N` and/or `?maxLength=N` to filter by character count of the paragraph text.

Look up a paragraph using any of three ID formats:
- **globalId**: "1:2.0.1" (partId:paperId.sectionId.paragraphId)
- **standardReferenceId**: "2:0.1" (paperId:sectionId.paragraphId)
- **paperSectionParagraphId**: "2.0.1" (paperId.sectionId.paragraphId)

The format is auto-detected from the reference string.

Use `?include=entities` to include typed entity mentions in the response.

get.summary: Get a paragraph with surrounding context
get.description:
Returns the target paragraph along with N paragraphs before and after it (ordered by sort_id).
Useful for AI agents doing RAG that need surrounding context for better understanding.
The `window` query parameter controls how many paragraphs before/after to include (default: 2, max: 10).

Use `?include=entities` to include typed entity mentions in the response.

post.description:
Search the Urantia Papers using full-text search. Supports three search modes:
- **and**: All words must appear (default)
- **or**: Any word can appear
- **phrase**: Exact phrase match

Results are ranked by relevance. Optional filters: paperId, partId.

get.description:
Search the Urantia Papers using full-text search. Supports three search modes:
- **and**: All words must appear (default)
- **or**: Any word can appear
- **phrase**: Exact phrase match

Results are ranked by relevance. Optional filters: paperId, partId.

Accepts query parameters instead of a JSON body. Designed for AI agents and browser-based access.

post.summary: Semantic similarity search across all paragraphs
post.description:
Search the Urantia Papers using semantic similarity (vector embeddings).
Returns conceptually related results even without exact keyword matches.
Optional filters: paperId, partId.

get.summary: Semantic similarity search across all paragraphs (GET)
get.description:
Search the Urantia Papers using semantic similarity (vector embeddings).
Returns conceptually related results even without exact keyword matches.
Optional filters: paperId, partId.

Accepts query parameters instead of a JSON body. Designed for AI agents and browser-based access.
Browse the entity catalog (beings, places, orders, races, religions, concepts). Supports filtering by type and searching by name.
Returns a single entity by its slug ID.
Returns all paragraphs that mention a given entity, ordered by position in the text.
Returns available languages with translation progress (entity and paragraph counts).

get.summary: Get audio URL for a paragraph
get.description: Returns the audio file URL for a given paragraph. Accepts any paragraph reference format (globalId, standardReferenceId, or paperSectionParagraphId).

get.summary: Format a citation for a passage
get.description:
Generate a formatted citation for any Urantia Book passage. Supports APA, MLA, Chicago, and BibTeX styles.

Reference formats accepted:
- **standardReferenceId**: "196:2.1" (paperId:sectionId.paragraphId)
- **globalId**: "1:196.2.1" (partId:paperId.sectionId.paragraphId)
- **paperSectionParagraphId**: "196.2.1" (paperId.sectionId.paragraphId)

get.summary: Generate a dynamic Open Graph image for a passage
get.description:
Returns a 1200×630 PNG Open Graph image for a Urantia Book passage. Designed for social media previews.

Optional `?theme=` parameter: `default` (blue), `warm` (amber), `purple`, `minimal` (no glow).

Images are cached permanently (`Cache-Control: immutable`).

Export embedding vectors for all paragraphs in a paper. The `paperId` query parameter is required.

Returns JSONL (default) or JSON. Each line/item contains `{ ref, embedding }`. A typical paper is 50-200 paragraphs (~1-5 MB).

get.summary: Get the embedding vector for a paragraph
get.description: Returns the 1536-dimensional embedding vector for a single paragraph. Useful for building custom similarity search or clustering.
